questions.text: What is the opening warning of Surah Al-Anbiya?
questions.answer: That the account (judgment) for mankind has drawn near, yet they turn away in heedlessness.

questions.text: What is the response to the claim that the heavens and earth could have multiple gods?
questions.answer: If there were gods other than Allah, the heavens and earth would have been ruined (in chaos).

questions.text: Describe the 'Big Bang' like concept mentioned in Verse 30.
questions.answer: That the heavens and the earth were a joined entity (Ratqan) and Allah separated them (Fataqnahuma).

questions.text: What is the origin of all living things according to Verse 30?
questions.answer: Water.

questions.text: What is the 'protected ceiling' mentioned in Verse 32?
questions.answer: The sky/atmosphere.

questions.text: How does Allah describe every soul's destiny regarding death?
questions.answer: Every soul shall taste death, and people are tested with evil and good as a trial.

questions.text: What did Ibrahim (AS) do to the idols of his people?
questions.answer: He smashed them into pieces, except for the largest one, to show their helplessness.

questions.text: How was Ibrahim (AS) saved from the fire his people threw him into?
questions.answer: Allah commanded the fire: 'O fire, be coolness and safety upon Ibrahim'.

questions.text: Which Prophet was given the ability to make iron soft and understand the birds?
questions.answer: Prophet Dawud (David).

questions.text: Which Prophet was given control over the wind and the jinn?
questions.answer: Prophet Sulayman (Solomon).

questions.text: What was the 'Cry' of Prophet Ayyub (Job) in his distress?
questions.answer: 'Indeed, adversity has touched me, and You are the Most Merciful of the merciful'.

questions.text: What was the 'Dua' of Yunus (AS) inside the belly of the whale?
questions.answer: La ilaha illa Anta, Subhanaka, inni kuntu minaz-zalimin (There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers).

questions.text: How does Allah describe Prophet Muhammad (SAW) in Verse 107?
questions.answer: As a 'Mercy to the worlds' (Rahmatan lil-'Alamin).

questions.text: What will happen to the heavens on the Day of Judgment (Verse 104)?
questions.answer: Allah will fold the heaven like the folding of a scroll for records.

questions.text: Who are 'Dhul-Kifl' and 'Idris' mentioned alongside Ismail?
questions.answer: They were among the patient ones (Sabirin).

questions.text: What does Allah say about the 'balance' of deeds on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: He will place the scales of justice, and no soul will be treated unjustly even by the weight of a mustard seed.

questions.text: What is the 'Al-Furqan' given to Musa and Harun?
questions.answer: The Criterion (the Torah) which was a light and a reminder for the righteous.

questions.text: What is the state of the 'earth' promised to the righteous servants?
questions.answer: That the earth will be inherited by His righteous servants.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the creation of humans in the context of haste?
questions.answer: Man was created of haste (instinctive impatience).

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say when the 'True Promise' (Judgment) draws near?
questions.answer: 'O woe to us; we were in heedlessness of this; rather, we were wrongdoers'.
